seo.title: Home Inspection Services MA | Ken Arnold Home Inspection
seo.description: Buyer, pre-listing, new construction, condo, and multi-family home inspections across the Pioneer Valley. MA Lic. #587. Fast digital reports.

sections.heading: Home Inspection Services in Massachusetts
sections.subheading: Find the Right Inspection for Your Situation
sections.intro: Not every inspection is the same — and the wrong scope can leave critical questions unanswered. Ken Arnold Home Inspection LLC offers six core residential inspection types, each matched to a specific transaction, property, and set of stakes. All performed personally by Ken (MA License #587), all delivered as a comprehensive photo-documented digital report.

paragraphs.html: The type of inspection that's right for you depends on your role in the transaction, the property type, and what's at stake. A buyer needs a different scope than a seller. A condo purchase has different considerations than a triple-decker investment. Getting the right inspection from the start means no gaps, no surprises.

Ken Arnold has been performing residential inspections across the Pioneer Valley for over 22 years. He can advise on the right scope for your specific situation — just call or book online and describe what you're working with.

link_list.label: Maintaining a home you already own

sections.heading: Six Inspection Types.
sections.intro: Every inspection Ken performs is conducted personally, follows ASHI Standards of Practice, and is documented in a comprehensive digital report with annotated photographs. The scope changes by property type — the standard doesn't.

cards.title: Buyer Home Inspection
cards.body: A thorough, independent inspection of every major system before you close — 22 years of Pioneer Valley experience documented in a detailed photo report.

cards.title: Pre-Listing Home Inspection
cards.body: Find out what a buyer's inspector will find — before you list. Get ahead of objections, price the home accurately, and shorten your time on market.

cards.title: New Construction Home Inspection
cards.body: An independent inspection before your warranty closes. Phase, pre-drywall, or final-walk inspections — finding what the builder's punch list misses.

cards.title: Condo & Townhouse Inspection
cards.body: Targeted inspection of your unit plus the shared-system review most condo buyers don't get — common to mill conversions, downtown buildings, and townhouse complexes across Western Mass.

cards.title: Multi-Family Home Inspection
cards.body: Triple-deckers, two-families, and four-unit investment properties — each unit and shared system documented separately for buyers and investors.

cards.title: Annual & Preventative Home Inspection
cards.body: Full-scope inspection for current owners — oriented to maintenance planning, not closing pressure. Worth doing every 3–5 years on older Pioneer Valley homes.

sections.heading: Beyond the Standard Inspection
sections.subheading: Add What the Property Actually Needs
sections.intro: Radon, water quality, wood-destroying insects, mold, and hidden moisture aren't detectable on a standard visual inspection — but they can be the most expensive surprises after closing. Ken offers a full range of specialty testing services, most of which can be added to any inspection visit.

cards.title: Mold Inspection
cards.body: Visual mold evaluation with moisture-source identification, optional air sampling. Common in older Pioneer Valley basements and mill-conversion units.

cards.title: Radon Testing
cards.body: 48-hour continuous-monitor radon testing in the Pioneer Valley. Add to any home inspection or schedule standalone.

cards.title: Termite & WDI Inspection
cards.body: NPMA-33 wood-destroying insect inspection — required for most VA, FHA, and many investor loans. Carpenter ant, carpenter bee, and powderpost beetle evaluation included.
cards.aria: Learn more about Termite & WDI Inspection

cards.title: Thermal Imaging Inspection
cards.body: Infrared scanning surfaces issues invisible to the naked eye — water intrusion, missing insulation, electrical hot spots, and HVAC performance problems.
cards.aria: Learn more about Thermal Imaging Inspection

cards.title: Well Water Testing
cards.body: Comprehensive pre-purchase well water testing for rural Pioneer Valley properties. Lab analysis through state-certified labs, results in 5–10 business days.
cards.aria: Learn more about Well Water Testing

cards.title: Septic System Inspection
cards.body: Pre-purchase septic system evaluation for rural Pioneer Valley properties. Coordinated with Massachusetts Title 5 inspectors when transfer requires it.
cards.aria: Learn more about Septic System Inspection

paragraphs.html: Whether you're buying a 1920s farmhouse in Deerfield, a new-build condo in Northampton, or a multi-family in Holyoke — every Ken Arnold inspection follows the same commitment: thorough, independent, and comprehensively documented.

checklist.text: MA License #587 — 22 years of active inspections across the Pioneer Valley
ASHI Standards of Practice — the highest benchmark in residential inspection
Every inspection personally conducted by Ken — no subcontractors
Photo-documented digital report — comprehensive, organized by priority
You're welcome on-site and encouraged to attend
No conflicts of interest — Ken works for you, not the transaction

items.q: Does Ken Arnold perform all inspections himself?
items.a: Yes. Ken personally conducts every inspection — no subcontractors, no dispatched employees. The same licensed professional who answers your call is the one on your roof, in your crawl space, and writing your report. That consistency matters, and it's rare.

items.q: What inspection types does Ken offer?
items.a: Ken performs six core residential inspection types: buyer home inspections, pre-listing (seller's) inspections, new construction inspections, condo and townhouse inspections, multi-family inspections, and annual preventative inspections. Specialty testing can be added to any visit.

items.q: Can I add specialty testing to any inspection?
items.a: Yes — most specialty services can be bundled into the same appointment. Commonly added services include radon testing, well water testing, termite and WDI inspection, thermal imaging, and mold inspection. Bundling saves time and gets you a complete picture of the property in one visit.

items.q: How long does an inspection take?
items.a: Most single-family residential inspections run 2.5–3 hours. Older homes, larger properties, and multi-family buildings take longer. New construction phase inspections vary by phase. Ken will give you a realistic time estimate when you book.

items.q: When will I receive my report?
items.a: Reports are delivered as a photo-documented digital PDF — typically that evening or the next morning. Weekend or back-to-back inspections may push delivery by a day or two. Either way, Ken's standard is always to get your report inside your due-diligence window.

items.q: What areas of Massachusetts does Ken serve?
items.a: Ken serves the Pioneer Valley, Connecticut River Valley, and surrounding communities — including Amherst, Northampton, Greenfield, Deerfield, Hadley, Easthampton, Holyoke, Chicopee, Sunderland, and more. See the full service area for details.

sections.heading: Not Sure Which Inspection You Need?
sections.subheading: Ken Can Help You Figure It Out.
sections.body: Call or book online and describe your situation — Ken will recommend the right inspection scope for your property, your role, and your timeline. No upsells, no pressure. Just straight answers from an independent licensed inspector.
sections.footnote: Licensed Home Inspector MA #587 · 35 Keets Rd, Deerfield, MA 01342 · Serving Franklin County, Hampshire County & the Pioneer Valley
